a: Type to represent a Boolean value.

These macros define convenient constants to represent Boolean values.

A set of methods that you can use to create and remove bindings between view objects and controllers, or between controllers and model objects.
A mechanism by which you can access the properties of an object indirectly by name or key.
An informal protocol that objects adopt to be notified of changes to the specified properties of other objects.
The root class of most Objective-C class hierarchies, from which subclasses inherit a basic interface to the runtime system and the ability to behave as Objective-C objects.
A Combine publisher that produces a new element whenever the observed value changes.
The group of methods that are fundamental to all Objective-C objects.

These macros define null values for classes and instances.
`OBJC_OLD_DISPATCH_PROTOTYPES == 0` enforces the rule that the dispatch functions must be cast to an appropriate function pointer type.

If you define an Objective-C root class, you receive a compiler error indicating that the class is defined without specifying a base class. You can avoid this compiler error by preceding the definition of the root class (that is, before the `@interface` directive) with `OBJC_ROOT_CLASS`.
This macro annotates a class as being an Objective-C root class.
Gain low-level access to the Objective-C runtime and the Objective-C root types.
Describes the macOS Objective-C runtime library support functions and data structures.
Defines an opaque type that represents a method selector.
A set of methods that accessibility elements can use to support specific actions.
A pair of properties to allow you to fine-tune how drags and drops are exposed to assistive technologies.
An informal protocol that provides a way to determine whether an assistive app, such as VoiceOver, has focus on an accessible element.
The `WebPlugIn` informal protocol defines methods that enable interaction between an application using the WebKit framework and any WebKit-based plug-ins it may use.
`WebPlugInContainer` is an informal protocol that enables a plug-in to send messages to the application.
`WebScripting` is an informal protocol that defines methods that classes can implement to export their interfaces to a WebScript environment such as JavaScript.
Defines `YES` as `1`.

An integer specifying the total number of allocated cache buckets (minus one). During method lookup, the Objective-C runtime uses this field to determine the index at which to begin a linear search of the `buckets` array. A pointer to a method’s selector is masked against this field using a logical AND operation (`index = (mask & selector))`. This serves as a simple hashing algorithm.

Sets the property of the receiver specified by a given key to a given value.
Sets the value for the property identified by a given key path to a given value.
Invoked by [setValue(_:forKey:)](/documentation/objectivec/nsobject-swift.class/setvalue(_:forkey:)) when it finds no property for a given key.
Sets properties of the receiver with values from a given dictionary, using its keys to identify the properties.
Sets the receiver’s version number.
Allows the delegate to specify which device is its preferred.
This delegate method allows the delegate to determine if the media inserted in the device is suitable for whatever operation is to be performed.
Allows the delegate to determine if device can be used as a target.
Sent by the default notification center when the device selection in the panel has changed.
This delegate method allows the delegate to control how media reservations are handled.
Sent to the delegate to determine whether the action should be enabled.
Returns the property identified by a given key.
A pointer to a [Class](/documentation/objectivec/class) data structure. Specifies the particular superclass of the instance to message.
Returns the class object for the receiver’s superclass.
Returns the class object for the receiver’s superclass.
Writes the specified rows to the specified pasteboard.
Sets the value of the property identified by a given key.
Sets the value for the property identified by `key` to `value`.
Sets the value for the property identified by `keyPath` to `value`.
Sets properties of the receiver with values from a given dictionary, using its keys to identify the properties
Sent to the delegate to request the title of the menu item for the action.
An array containing the keys for the to-many relationship properties of the receiver.
The keys for the to-one relationship properties of the receiver, if any.
The types of the method arguments.
Invoked if `key` is represented by a scalar attribute.
Removes a given binding between the receiver and a controller.

Returns `true` if the stored value methods [storedValue(forKey:)](/documentation/objectivec/nsobject-swift.class/storedvalue(forkey:)) and [takeStoredValue(_:forKey:)](/documentation/objectivec/nsobject-swift.class/takestoredvalue(_:forkey:)) should use private accessor methods in preference to public accessors.
Returns the mode mask corresponding to the expected font panel mode.
Implemented to override the default action of enabling or disabling a specific menu item.
If this method is implemented and returns `false`, NSToolbar will disable `theItem`; returning `true` causes `theItem` to be enabled.
Indicates whether the value specified by a given pointer is valid, or can be made valid, for the property identified by a given key.
Indicates whether the value specified by a given pointer is not valid for a given key path relative to the receiver.
The value of the attribute (usually empty).
Retrieves an indexed object from the collection specified by the passed key.
Returns the value for the property identified by a given key.
Returns the value for the derived property identified by a given key path.
Invoked by [value(forKey:)](/documentation/objectivec/nsobject-swift.class/value(forkey:)) when it finds no property corresponding to a given key.
Retrieves a named object from the collection specified by the passed key.
Retrieves an object by ID from the collection specified by the passed key.
Returns the class of the value that will be returned for the specified binding.
Returns a dictionary containing as keys the property names in `keys`, with corresponding values being the corresponding property values.
Returns the version number assigned to the class.
Returns the tool tip string to be displayed due to the cursor pausing at location `point` within the tool tip rectangle identified by `tag` in the view `view`.
Returns the `WebFrame` that contains the plug-in.
Loads a URL into a web frame.
Returns the plug-in selection color.
Tells the container to show a status message.
Prepares the plug-in for deallocation.
Initializes the plug-in.
Invoked when an error occurs loading the main resource.
Invoked when the connection successfully finishes loading data.
Invoked when the connection receives sufficient data to construct the URL response for its request.
Invoked when the connection loads data incrementally.
Controls plug-in behavior based on its selection.
Tells the plug-in to start normal operation.
Tells the plug-in to stop normal operation.
Returns the scripting environment name for a selector.
Returns the scripting environment name for an attribute specified by a key.
Informs the observed object that the specified change is about to be executed at given indexes for a specified ordered to-many relationship.
Informs the observed object that the value of a given property is about to change.
Informs the observed object that the specified change is about to be made to a specified unordered to-many relationship.
Zones are deprecated and ignored by most classes that have it as a parameter.
